kept.text:
SpaceX wants to build a $55 billion next-generation, vertically integrated semiconductor manufacturing and advanced computing fabrication facility in Grimes county, Texas, according to a new filing.

• Proposed investment: $55B upfront, up to $119B total
• Focus: semiconductor manufacturing + advanced computing
• Location: Grimes County, TX

From the filing: "SpaceX proposes construction of a multi-phase, next-generation, vertically integrated semiconductor manufacturing and advanced computing fabrication facility, which would represent a transformative investment in domestic semiconductor manufacturing capacity. Estimated capital investment for the initial phases is $55 billion, with an estimated total capital investment (if additional phases are constructed) of $119 billion."

The Grimes County Commissioners Court is scheduled to hold a public hearing at 9 AM on June 3, 2026, at the Grimes County Justice & Business Center, to consider approval of a property tax abatement agreement.

Grimes County is a little over 2 hours from Austin.

Source: https://t.co/alpJvve1IS

kept.text:
Elon Musk: The Roadster will have rockety stuff.

Elon: “The Roadster will be a collaboration between SpaceX and Tesla. You can expect some rockety stuff there.” 

Don Lemon: “A flying car?” 

Elon: “Maybe. It's not out of the question. It's going to be really cool. It's going to have some rocket technology in it. Really, the only way to do something that's cooler than the Cybertruck is to combine SpaceX and Tesla technology to create something that's not even really a car, something that's never existed before.”

Don Lemon: “I'm getting Jetsons vibes.” 

Elon: “Totally Jetsons vibes. It'll do 0 to 60 in under one second. So that's by far faster than any sports car that exists. And that's not even the most exciting thing about it. It'll have a drive-by-wire yoke essentially like the way aircraft or modern jets are controlled. I don't think anyone will ever make anything like the Roadster that we're going to make.”

Interview with Don Lemon, March 16, 2024

kept.text:
Elon Musk: 

“It’s better to err on the side of optimism and be wrong than err on the side of pessimism and be right for quality of life.” https://t.co/oVBcGJDLJf

🚨 SpaceX is gearing up for a staggering $55 billion expansion into semiconductor manufacturing and advanced computing 💥

A new filing reveals plans for a cutting-edge, vertically integrated fabrication facility in Grimes County, signaling a huge leap into building the silicon that will power the future of the company.

The proposed site sits within a newly designated reinvestment zone near the Gibbons Creek Reservoir off Highway 30, roughly twenty miles east of Bryan-College Station. 

As confirmed by Elon Musk, Grimes County is one of several locations currently under consideration for what he described as the largest and most advanced chip fabrication facility in the world.

This direct acknowledgment adds major weight to the speculation that this mega-site is tied to Terafab—the monumental joint venture between SpaceX, Tesla, xAI, and Intel. Designed to completely internalize the supply chain for next-generation AI and advanced computing, the facility's total capital investment could reach an astonishing $119 billion if all subsequent phases are constructed.

Local leaders are already moving quickly to attract the massive build. The Grimes County Commissioners Court is scheduled to hold a public hearing on June 3 to discuss a crucial property tax abatement agreement, which could be a deciding factor in securing the transformative project for the region.

kept.text:
SpaceX Just Unleashed the Insane Specs for Starship V4 — And It’s Next-Level!
Hey space fans! Elon Musk just dropped fresh details on Starship Version 4 (targeted for 2027), and it’s clear: the future of spaceflight just got a whole lot bigger, bolder, and more powerful.If you thought Starship was already a beast, V4 is about to redefine what’s https://t.co/kb42D9QPwJ’s the mind-blowing upgrade package: 42 Raptor Engines Total
Super Heavy booster stays at 33 engines, but the upper stage gets stretched and upgraded to 9 engines (3 sea-level + 6 vacuum-optimized). As Elon put it: “as foretold in the prophecy.” 10,000 Metric Tons of Thrust
That’s roughly three times the liftoff thrust of the mighty Saturn V. This will be the most powerful rocket ever built by humanity. Towering Height: Nearly 150 Meters
Imagine stacking three Statues of Liberty on top of each other. That’s how tall this monster will stand. Game-Changing Payload
Designed to deliver 200+ metric tons to Low Earth Orbit in fully reusable mode. This slashes the cost of getting mass to space and opens the door to real Mars colonies and massive orbital infrastructure. Flying Space Station
The upper stage will offer over 1,000 cubic meters of pressurized volume — that’s more internal living space than the entire International Space Station!Why go this big?As Starship gained heat shields, reinforcements, and all the real-world hardware needed for safety and reusability, it got heavier. Instead of endlessly chasing weight reductions, SpaceX chose to scale up everything: longer tanks, more propellant, more engines. The result? A rocket that can still haul the massive payloads required for humanity’s multi-planetary future.They’re still crushing it with V3 first — a huge leap on its own. V3 should wrap up production and testing by the end of this year, with heavy flight operations throughout 2026–2027 before V4 takes center https://t.co/8gH2ZsrYls… with Starship V4 basically being a flying city block with more living space than the ISS, would you sign up for a multi-month journey to Mars aboard one? Or are you watching safely from Earth? Drop your thoughts below — I’d love to hear them!Note: This image is an illustrative render for educational purposes, not an official SpaceX image.

kept.text:
Elon Musk: “When I started SpaceX, one of my closest friends got a compilation of rocket failures and made me watch the whole thing. Lots of people tried to talk me out of it. The joke was: how do you make a small fortune in the rocket business? You start with a large one.

I heard that joke so many times that I’d just say the punchline myself. People would ask, ‘Why did you start a rocket company?’ and I’d say I was trying to figure out the fastest way to turn a large fortune into a small one.

But I already thought the probability of failure was high and the likelihood of success was low. I thought SpaceX maybe had a 10–20% chance of success.”

kept.text:
$MCHP 

방사선 내성 메모리 분야의 수요가 조금씩 확인되고 있습니다. 

2026년 하반기 골든돔과 SpaceX IPO가 반도체 섹터의 상당히 중요한 모멘텀이 될 수 있습니다. 

언제나, 조용히 성장하는 기업에 주목할 필요가 있습니다. https://t.co/vT9TKhb1fC

profile_bio.description:
지정학, 우주, AI를 비롯한 모든 것을 공부합니다. 
$RKLB $TSLA $PLTR
구독에서 주로 활동합니다.

quoted_tweet.text:
내가 오래전부터, HPSC 프로젝트로 관심 가지던 기업 

$MCHP 

우주 산업, 특히 방사선 내성(Radiation Tolerant) 메모리 분야에서 NASA와 협력하는 대표적인 기업임.

AI 시대에 뒤떨어진다고 무시받고 있지만, 다음 사이클인 우주에서는 과연 무시 받을까?

잘 생각해보자. 우주에서 작동하는 물건이 많아지면 필연적으로 우주에서 사용 될 반도체 수도 증가함.

과거 우주 산업은 성능이 낮더라도 방사선에 완벽하게 견디는 'Rad-Hard(Radiation Hardened)' 칩을 사용했음. 

하지만 AI, 고해상도 위성 영상 처리 등 우주에서도 고성능 컴퓨팅이 필요해지면서, 마이크론의 최신 공정 메모리가 주목받고 있음.

우주 방사선이 반도체에게 가장 치명적임.

방사선 내성(Radiation Tolerant, RT) 기술은 $MCHP의 가장 강력한 해자

MCHP가 Microsemi를 인수하면서 RT PolarFire FPGA 기술을 확보하게 됨. 

경쟁사(Xilinx, Altera) 대비 전력 소모가 50% 수준으로 매우 낮은게 특징. 

전력 공급이 제한적인 위성(특히 LEO 군집 위성)에서 '발열 관리'와 '전력 효율'은 생명과도 같기 때문에, 최근 뉴스페이스 위성들이 가장 선호하는 FPGA 중 하나임.

NASA와의 계약 및 협력 (HPSC 프로젝트)

이게 내가 가장 중요하게 보는 핵심. 

NASA는 마이크론을 '고성능 우주 비행 컴퓨팅(HPSC, High-Performance Spaceflight Computing)' 프로젝트의 핵심 파트너 선정.

2022년 NASA JPL은 차세대 우주선용 프로세서 개발 파트너로 $MCHP를 선정 (약 5,000만 달러 규모)

목표는 단 하나.

현재의 우주 컴퓨터보다 약 100배 빠른 처리 속도를 가지면서도 전력 소모는 줄이는 컴퓨팅 플랫폼 개발.

향후 10~20년 동안 발사될 NASA 및 상업용 위성의 '두뇌 표준'을 $MCHP가 장악한다는 뜻

그리고 골든돔. 

우주 방사선 내성 기술은 핵폭발이나 전자기펄스(EMP) 상황을 대비해야 하는 국방(미사일, 군사위성) 분야의 수요와 직결.

어쩔 수 없이 앞으로 커질 수 밖에 없는 시장이라는거지.

정리해보면 

MCHP는 '올드 스페이스(NASA, 방산)'가 요구하는 극한의 신뢰성(Rad-Hard)과 '뉴스페이스(SpaceX, RKLB)'가 요구하는 가성비(Rad-Tolerant, COTS) 라인업을 모두 갖추고 있음.

->사실상 독점 수준이라는 거지. 

HPSC 프로젝트는 엣지 컴퓨팅(위성 자체에서 데이터 처리)을 가능하게 함. (이게 골든돔이랑 연결)

2025~2026년은 HPSC 프로세서가 실제 엔지니어링 샘플 단계를 넘어 실질적인 탑재 논의가 구체화되는 시기

AI 시대가 저물고 우주 섹터가 조금씩 고개를 들때, 

반도체를 투자해야겠다면 나는 여기도 관심목록에 추가해야한다고 봄.

kept.text:
$ASTS The 'Google *could* sell ASTS to buy Space X stock' headline is farcical, not surprisingly - as Farrar said it and Stocktwits propagated it. 
 
Equally, Google *could* sell Space X to buy ASTS. Google already owns a  big chunk of Space X and I suspect it won't wait for a public IPO to buy more. Why would it? 
 
In fact, it seems more plausible Google would cash out (could make a good $100 B if the $2 T valuation holds). Then, frankly, it could make a bid for all of ASTS! 
 
Ultimately, Google wants investments in this space to safeguard its obvious interests in internet connectivity. If Starlink is shown up to be inadequate for those purposes, whilst ASTS outperforms its peers, frankly I think the direction of investment flows is pretty obvious.
